text.title: User Research at Scale Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the User Research at Scale methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Coding MUST apply a frozen codebook; novel themes go to a proposed_codes overflow channel for weekly review, never auto-applied.
rule.rationale: Auto-extending the codebook produces theme drift; the overflow channel preserves novelty without polluting the book.

rule.statement: Any published finding MUST be supported by >=50 coded observations across >=10 sessions and >=2 segments.
rule.rationale: Below the floor, findings reflect noise or coding bias.

rule.statement: Findings MUST pass a human-in-the-loop review before publish; no auto-publish.
rule.rationale: AI coding mistakes propagate; HITL catches them.

rule.statement: Interview transcripts and any PII MUST be processed through ZDR-eligible LLM endpoints only.
rule.rationale: Privacy + DSA compliance; non-ZDR retention is a legal liability.

rule.statement: Transcripts MUST be speaker-attributed; interviewer turns are stripped before coding.
rule.rationale: Coding interviewer prompts as user statements inflates false themes.

rule.statement: All writes to Dovetail/Notion/CRM via API MUST use idempotency keys (hash of source + date).
Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec — decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
